what_it_does: Implements a reusable retirement component for member sessions and channels: it crystallizes a session's perspective (member retire) or a channel's totality (channel retire) into corpus records, files linked board notes, enforces honest-state tagging on claims, checks harvest coverage before a channel closes, and only archives a channel when explicitly requested.
observations: Two retirement verbs: harvest_member for a single session and retire_channel for a whole channel.
Every claim in harvest_member must carry a status from VALID_STATUS ('verified','attempted-unverified','broken','abandoned'); missing tags raise RetireError.
harvest_status builds a coverage ledger by checking whether each channel member appears in a harvest filename or has authored a board item.
retire_channel gathers members, harvest files, attachment manifest counts, and board items into one channel-retirement corpus record.
Attachment references in the board item are capped at 50 per attachment type.
Channel archiving is strictly opt-in via archive=True and never happens as a side effect.
Imports runtime.corpus, runtime.cc_board, runtime.cc_attachments, and runtime.cc_channels as internal aliases.
Uses a fixed HARVEST_DIR under build-prep/the-one-application/harvest.
standouts: Honest-state enforcement on claims — no self-certified 'done' into the corpus.
Explicit, fail-loud coverage check before channel retirement.
Explicit opt-in archiving, with a strong comment that live channels are not closed as a side effect.
Cap on attachment edge fan-out to keep board item size bounded.
conventions: Heavy docstrings explaining intent and anti-patterns.
Type hints on public function signatures.
Internal helpers and imports prefixed with underscore.
Default project='company' parameter on public functions.
Constants in UPPER_CASE at module level.
concerns: Coverage matching uses substring membership (m in f), which can be ambiguous if handles are substrings of each other or filenames.
HARVEST_DIR is hard-coded and not parameterized per project or channel.
retire_channel only checks harvest files by substring and reverse board authorship, not by corpus record addresses.
Attachment links are capped at 50 per type with no marker that the list is truncated.
No validation of the store object's shape or contents.
board_items are found by listing all items and filtering by channel, which may not scale.
notes: A fail-loud, provenance-heavy retirement harness that tries to keep false 'done' claims out of permanent memory while preserving session and channel perspective.
questions: What is the exact shape and contract of the store argument passed to corpus.write_record?
What do corpus.write_record and cc_board.file_item return, and what are the address formats?
How are harvest filenames actually associated with a specific member and session?
What does cc_channels.channel_members return, and what does cc_channels.archive_channel do?
Is HARVEST_DIR intended to be shared across all projects or scoped per project?
The docstring mentions session_recall, but it is not used in this file — where is that integration expected?
What additional fields beyond text and status are expected inside each claim dict?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides reusable member and channel retirement with honest-state enforcement, coverage checking, corpus ingest, linked board records, and explicit archiving.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the wind-down of sessions and channels into a repeatable, trustworthy 'harvest' verb that preserves perspective and prevents false confidence from entering permanent memory.
novelty: Combines both member-perspective and channel-totality retirement in one component, with a small fixed taxonomy of honest-state claim statuses and a coverage ledger that must be confronted before a channel can be retired.
contribution: Provides a disciplined retirement workflow—honest-state claim validation, coverage checking, corpus record creation, linked board records, and explicit archive opt-in—that a fused system could reuse when closing out sessions or channels.
summary_for_embedding: A Python runtime module that implements member and channel retirement by validating honest-state claim tags, writing retirement corpus records, filing linked board notes, checking harvest coverage, and optionally archiving channels.
intention_for_embedding: To make session and channel shutdown a repeatable, attributable harvest that preserves perspective and blocks self-certified 'done' claims from entering permanent memory.

symbols.does: Custom exception that surfaces retirement failures loudly instead of silently no-oping.
symbols.description: RetireError subclasses RuntimeError and is raised whenever a retirement operation cannot proceed, reflecting the file's fail-loud design.

symbols.does: Lists .md files in the fixed HARVEST_DIR, returning an empty list if the directory does not exist.
symbols.description: A private helper used by harvest_status and retire_channel to discover existing harvest artifacts on disk.

symbols.does: Builds a coverage ledger for a channel by checking which members have a harvest file or board authorship evidence.
symbols.description: Returns a dict with members, harvested, missing, and complete flags, giving a fail-loud coverage proof before a channel is retired.

symbols.does: Validates honest-state tags on a session's claims and writes a retirement-member corpus record plus a linked board note.
symbols.description: Enforces that every claim carries one of the VALID_STATUS values, then persists the session's perspective into the corpus and files an attributed, linked board item.

symbols.does: Crystallizes a channel's totality into a corpus record and board note, with optional explicit archiving.
symbols.description: Gathers members, harvests, attachment manifest counts, and board items into one channel-retirement record, records coverage gaps, links up to 50 attachments per type, and only archives the channel when archive=True.
